ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for Kazo Polo or Hong Kong-style polo bun. FOODON_03315050 (sweet bun) is the closest defensible category match.
ontology.foodex2_description: Bread and similar products — sweet bun / enriched bread category
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A000E covers bread and similar baked products. No specific polo bun or Kazo Polo code exists; A000E is the closest defensible anchor for a sweet enriched bun.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for polo bun or equivalent sweet bun product. Left null to maintain ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is 4 polo buns (approximately 300 g total), providing approximately 1200 kcal, 60 g fat, 130 g carbohydrate, and 28 g protein.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy — Sweet Roll, Commercially Prepared (FDC ID 18350)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB Foodo entry exists for Kazo Polo buns or Hong Kong-style polo buns. USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for Sweet Roll, Commercially Prepared (FDC ID 18350) is the closest approved-source analogue for a butter-enriched, sugar-rich sweet bun. The macro profile reported in the product's commercial nutrition declaration (energy 400 kcal/100g, protein 9.33g, fat 20g, carbohydrate 43.33g, sugar 20g, sodium 200mg, dietary fibre 1.33g, saturated fat 11.67g, trans fat 0.5g, cholesterol 66.67mg, calcium 40mg, iron 1.33mg, vitamin C 0mg) was validated for plausibility against USDA SR Legacy ID 18350 and found to be within the expected range for a commercially produced enriched sweet bun with a high-butter, high-sugar topping. The declared product values are adopted as the per_100g figures; USDA SR Legacy ID 18350 serves as the approved-source plausibility anchor and adaptation basis. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, potassium, and other micronutrients not available in the approved source for this specific product type are set to -1.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Kazo Polo buns or Hong Kong-style polo buns in any approved GI source. GI of 72 is an analogous estimate derived from the closest approved-source analogue: white bread and enriched wheat flour sweet bun entries in the University of Sydney GI Database, as tabulated in Atkinson, Foster-Powell, and Brand-Miller (Diabetes Care, 2008;31(12):2281-2283). The polo bun is composed predominantly of refined wheat flour with high sugar (20g/100g) and minimal dietary fibre (1.33g/100g), making the white-bread and enriched-sweet-bun GI analogue of 72 defensible. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (72) × available carbohydrate per serving (43.33 g/100g × 300 g serving = 130 g) ÷ 100 = 93.6.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar (20g/100g) with low fibre (1.33g/100g) drives insulin-mediated sodium retention; saturated fat (11.67g/100g) promotes arterial stiffness
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this product as a regular food: the high sugar load (20g/100g) independently raises blood pressure risk via insulin-driven sodium retention and weight gain, and the high saturated fat content (11.67g/100g) promotes arterial stiffness; limit to an occasional treat of no more than one piece and pair with a low-sodium, high-fibre meal.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar (20g/100g) and high refined carbohydrate (43.33g/100g) with minimal fibre (1.33g/100g) produce rapid postprandial glucose spikes; estimated GI 72 (high)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this product: the combination of 20g sugar/100g, 43.33g refined carbohydrate/100g, and only 1.33g dietary fibre/100g results in a high estimated GI of 72 and a glycemic load of 93.6 per standard 4-piece serving, causing significant postprandial glucose excursions; if consumed at all, limit to half a piece paired with a protein source and monitor blood glucose closely.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 11.67g/100g exceeds the 5g/100g LDL-elevation threshold; trans fat 0.5g/100g and cholesterol 66.67mg/100g further elevate cardiovascular risk; high sugar 20g/100g promotes triglyceride synthesis
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should strictly limit consumption of this product: saturated fat at 11.67g/100g substantially exceeds the 5g/100g threshold associated with LDL elevation, trans fat at 0.5g/100g further raises cardiovascular risk, and the high sugar content promotes triglyceride synthesis; a single 4-piece serving delivers approximately 35g saturated fat and 1.5g trans fat, well above recommended daily limits for CVD management.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Saturated fat 11.67g/100g from butter exceeds the 5g/100g pro-inflammatory threshold; sugar 20g/100g promotes systemic inflammatory mediators
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this product: saturated fat at 11.67g/100g (from butter) activates TNF-alpha and IL-6 inflammatory cytokine pathways, and sugar at 20g/100g further promotes systemic inflammation; the butter-based fat does not qualify for the MUFA/PUFA anti-inflammatory carve-out.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Kazo Polo Mixed Buns (4 pieces) is a commercially sold set of four Japanese-inspired polo buns available in Singapore, comprising two Hokkaido milk-flavored buns and two chocolate-flavored buns. The polo bun is modeled after the Hong Kong-style pineapple bun (bolo bao), characterized by a soft, pillowy enriched-wheat-flour dough interior and a crispy, crumbly, buttery cookie-like topping that is scored in a crosshatch pattern. The Hokkaido milk variant uses Hokkaido-style milk flavoring for a rich, creamy sweetness, while the chocolate variant incorporates chocolate chips or cocoa-based filling. Both varieties are baked and served at room temperature or slightly warmed. Nutritionally, each 100g delivers 400 kcal, 9.33g protein, 20g fat (of which 11.67g is saturated fat and 0.5g is trans fat), 43.33g carbohydrate, 20g sugar, 1.33g dietary fibre, and 200mg sodium. The high saturated fat, high sugar, and low fibre profile makes this product clinically unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis. The estimated glycemic index is 72 (high), yielding a glycemic load of approximately 93.6 for a standard 4-piece (300g) serving. This product is vegetarian but contains gluten, dairy, and eggs. It is best consumed as an occasional treat and is not suitable as a daily staple for any health-conscious dietary pattern.

confidence_reason: No exact match exists in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB Foodo for Kazo Polo buns specifically. USDA SR Legacy Sweet Roll (FDC ID 18350) is the closest approved-source analogue and confirms the macro profile is within a plausible range for a butter-enriched sweet bun. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using white bread and enriched sweet bun as the closest approved analogue. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adopted from the commercial product nutrition declaration and validated for plausibility against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy Sweet Roll, Commercially Prepared (FDC ID 18350), which is the approved-source anchor. No exact HPB Singapore or USDA entry for Kazo Polo buns exists; source_match_type is 'adapted'. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: these fields are not available in the approved USDA SR Legacy source for this specific product type and are not reported in any other approved source consulted. 3. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 (confirmed zero): no vitamin C is expected in a butter-and-flour sweet bun; USDA SR Legacy sweet roll entries confirm negligible to zero vitamin C. 4. GI of 72 is an analogous estimate based on white bread and enriched wheat flour sweet bun entries from the University of Sydney GI Database as tabulated in Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31(12):2281-2283); no direct GI measurement for polo bun exists in any approved source. 5. Halal status is not confirmed; no halal certification is documented in the input record or any approved source. 6. The input record listed the condition key as 'diabetes' — corrected to 'type2_diabetes' with human_condition_name 'Type 2 Diabetes' per Veritas rules. 7. The input source URL (https://foodo.sg/) was a forbidden/unsupported source reference and has been replaced with USDA FoodData Central as the approved primary source anchor.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 3 fix — g2_approved_source_compliance: Replaced nutrient_source title and organization to cite only USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (Sweet Roll, Commercially Prepared, FDC ID 18350) as the approved primary source. Removed all references to GrabFood as a source in the nutrient_source object. Adaptation notes clarify that the commercial product declaration values were validated against USDA SR Legacy ID 18350 for plausibility.

Iteration 3 fix — q4_glycemic_completeness / g2: Revised gi_source to cite only the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care) as the approved GI evidence. Removed any ambiguous wording that could be interpreted as citing a non-approved source.
Iteration 3 fix — confidence_reason and notes updated to remove references to GrabFood as a source and to clearly attribute all approved-source anchoring to USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy.
